folio.label: 131r
folio.l: There witú he was , if men shulde hi~ discryue
On of the best faryngö man , on lyue
ñongö , strongö , rigút vertuous , and riche and wys
And welbyloued , and holden in gret prys
And shortly , if the sothe , I tellen shal
Vnwetyngö of this , Dorigen at al
This lusty Squyere , Seruaunt to venus
Whicú that I cleped was , Aurelius
Hadde loued hire best , of any creature
Two ©ere and more , as was his auenture
But neuäe durst he tellen hire , his greuaunce
Witú outen cuppe he drank , al his penaunce
He was dispeyred , nothyngö durst he seye
Saue in hise songes , somwhat wolde he wreye
His woo , as in a gentil compleynyngö
He seide he loued , and was byloued no thyngö
Of whicú matere , made he many layes
Songes , compleyntes , roundels , virelayes
How that he durst nat , his sorwe telle
But languysshetú , as a fire dotú in helle
And deye he must , he seide , as dide Ekko
For Narcisus , that durst nat telle hire wo
In othere manere , than ©e heere me seye
Ne durst he nat to hire , his woo bewreye
Saue that ¹auenture , somtyme at daunces
There ©onge folk kepen , here obseruaunces
It may wel be , he loked on hire face
In swicú a wyse , as man that asketú grace
But no thyngö wist she , of his entent
Nathelees it happed , er they thens went
By cause that he was , hire neighebour
And was a man of worshipe , and honour
And hadde I knowen hiö , of tyme ©ore
They fel in speche , and fortú more and more
Vn to that purpos , drougú Aurelius
And whan he saw his tyme , he seide thus
¢ Madame q¶ he , by god that this world made
If that I wiste , it mygút ©oure hert glade
I wolde that day , that ©oure Arueragus
Went ouä the See , that I Aurelius
Hadde went , there neuäe I shulde haue come a geyn
For wel I woot , my seruyse is in veyn
My gerdon is , but brestyngö of myn herte
Madame , rewetú vp on , my peynes smerte
For witú a word , ©e moun me slen or saue
